title: corydalis caseana subsp. cusickii
intro: Corydalis caseana subsp. cusickii is a perennial plant, also known as Western Corydalis, that belongs to the poppy family and is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. The plant contains a variety of alkaloids, including corydaline, which has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties to treat pain, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Corydalis caseana subsp. cusickii include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, improve respiratory function, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. In herbal preparations, Corydalis caseana subsp. cusickii is often used in teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
